\text{Step 1: Dive in with the Law of Sines to find the first angle } B_1.

\frac{\sin(B)}{b} = \frac{\sin(A)}{a}

\sin(B) = \frac{12.7\sin(26^\circ)}{6.9} \approx 0.8069

B_1 = \sin^{-1}(0.8069) \approx 53.8^\circ

\text{Step 2: Check if a second triangle is possible.}

\text{The theoretical second angle is } B_2 = 180^\circ - 53.8^\circ = 126.2^\circ.

\text{Check if it fits with the given angle } A: 26^\circ + 126.2^\circ = 152.2^\circ.

\text{Since } 152.2^\circ < 180^\circ \text{, there is room for a third angle! There are exactly TWO valid triangles.}

\text{Step 3: Solve Triangle 1 (using } B_1 \text{).}

\angle C_1 = 180^\circ - \angle A - B_1 \approx 180^\circ - 26^\circ - 53.8^\circ = 100.2^\circ

c_1 = \frac{a\sin(C_1)}{\sin(A)}

c_1 = \frac{6.9\sin(100.2^\circ)}{\sin(26^\circ)} \approx 15.49

\text{Step 4: Solve Triangle 2 (using } B_2 \text{).}

\angle C_2 = 180^\circ - \angle A - B_2 \approx 180^\circ - 26^\circ - 126.2^\circ = 27.8^\circ

c_2 = \frac{a\sin(C_2)}{\sin(A)}

c_2 = \frac{6.9\sin(27.8^\circ)}{\sin(26^\circ)} \approx 7.34
